title: Arachnothryx brevicorolla
taxonomy: Arachnothryx brevicorolla is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This family is a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as the madder family or coffee family. Within the family Rubiaceae, Arachnothryx brevicorolla is placed in the genus Arachnothryx, which is a group of flowering plants found primarily in the Americas. The Rubiaceae family is further classified into several subfamilies, and Arachnothryx brevicorolla is part of the subfamily Cinchonoideae. The taxonomic classification of Arachnothryx brevicorolla reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other plants in the Rubiaceae family.

intro: Arachnothryx brevicorolla, also known as the Short-cupped Arachnothryx, is a flowering plant species in the family Rubiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, colds, and respiratory issues, due to its potential health benefits in reducing inflammation and promoting immune function. The bioactive constituents of Arachnothryx brevicorolla include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and diterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in folk remedies to prepare cough syrups and expectorants.
